commit.message:
fix(ss-inbound): loop on UDP decrypt error to re-register waker (#1309)

* fix(ss-inbound): loop on UDP decrypt error to re-register waker

When poll_recv_from_with_ctrl returns Poll::Ready(Err(e)) — e.g. an
SS2022 authentication failure from scan traffic or a brief password
mismatch during config refresh — the waker registered by the previous
poll_recv_from call was already consumed.  Returning Poll::Pending
without a new waker permanently suspends the UDP dispatch task (t1):
no further packets are ever processed, recv_q fills up, and every
subsequent UDP probe times out.

Fix: wrap the recv/match in a loop.  On error we fall through to the
next iteration; poll_recv_from_with_ctrl either reads the next queued
packet immediately or, if the socket is empty, returns Poll::Pending
task until new data arrives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dispatcher): preserve original dst IP before reverse_lookup for UDP

When reverse_lookup converts a packet's destination IP back to a domain
name (via the DNS cache), family_hint_for_session loses the original
address family and falls back to resolve_v6, which can return an IPv6
address for an originally-IPv4 destination.  This causes new_udp_socket
to create an IPv6 socket while bind_addr is still 0.0.0.0 (derived from
the IPv4 inbound source), resulting in EINVAL on bind.

Concrete case: TCP probe resolves cp.cloudflare.com -> 1.1.1.1 and
caches it.  UDP probe relays to 1.1.1.1:53; reverse_lookup finds
'cp.cloudflare.com' in cache -> domain; family_hint_for_session calls
resolve_v6('cp.cloudflare.com') -> 2606:4700:4700::1111 -> IPv6 socket
-> bind(0.0.0.0:0) -> EINVAL every minute.

Fix: stash the original destination IP in sess.resolved_ip before
reverse_lookup runs.  family_hint_for_session already checks
resolved_ip first and returns it directly, so it never reaches the
resolve_v6 fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ss inbound: replace peek_user_identity with post-handshake user_key()

Use shadowsocks-rust fork (Watfaq/shadowsocks-rust@dee2d932) which
exposes CryptoStream::user_key() and ProxyServerStream::user_key().

After the SS2022 handshake, look up the authenticated user by matching
socket.user_key() against the user manager — no manual BLAKE3 KDF +
AES-256-ECB peek needed. Removes the aes and blake3 dependencies from
the inbound TCP path and works for any cipher, not just
2022-blake3-aes-256-gcm.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(ss-inbound): fix nightly rustfmt formatting in datagram.rs

docs: add instruction file patterns to VS Code LSP document selector (#466)

* docs: add instruction file patterns to VS Code LSP document selector

The instructions/oversized rule (added in v0.20.0) checks CLAUDE.md,
AGENTS.md, COPILOT.md, GEMINI.md, INSTRUCTIONS.md, and *.instructions.md
files, but these patterns were missing from the VS Code extension's
documentSelector. Without them, VS Code does not route did_open/did_save
events to the aipm LSP for those files, so real-time diagnostics from
instructions/oversized are never delivered in the editor.

Changes:
- vscode-aipm/src/extension.ts: add six instruction file glob patterns
  to the documentSelector so VS Code activates the language server for
  these files and publishes diagnostics on open and save
- docs/guides/vscode-extension.md: extend the "Supported file types"
  table with all six instruction file patterns
- README.md: update the "Supported file patterns" inline list to match

feat(executor-dbos)!: caller owns DBOS lifecycle (#22)

Remove init_dbos(). The caller now constructs DBOS() and calls
DBOS.launch() themselves, so they can register their own @DBOS.workflow
/ @DBOS.scheduled decorators against the same instance before launch.

Importing the package still applies the SQLite migration patch (now
exposed as apply_sqlite_patch) and imports the workflow/step modules so
their decorators register with DBOS before the caller's launch.

BREAKING CHANGE: init_dbos() removed. Callers must construct and launch
DBOS themselves. exoclaw-nanobot needs a follow-up PR to stop calling
init_dbos and move construction/launch to its own caller.

Add sd_assert_sig_equals_val_n_cycles_after_condition checker

General-purpose conditional value checker: verifies that a signal equals
a specific value exactly NUM_CYCLES clock cycles after a condition is true.

Covers common verification patterns:
- Reset output verification (NUM_CYCLES=0, value='0)
- Soft-reset register clear (NUM_CYCLES=1, value='0)
- Write-source collision default branch (NUM_CYCLES=1, value='0)
- Generic delayed response checking (any NUM_CYCLES)

The describeSchedule() and describeScheduleCompact() functions used
timeToMins() which drops seconds, causing sub-minute durations (e.g.
20s clip × 1 loop) to show as '24 hrs ⚠️ Spans midnight' instead of
'20 sec'. Switched all duration calculations to timeToSecs() and added
sub-minute formatting (N sec, M min N sec).

Also updated getLoopInfoHtml() to accept seconds directly instead of
converting from minutes.

fix(security): resolve python unpinned vulnerabilities (#28)

* fix: use NPM OIDC trusted publisher instead of token

The publish workflow was failing with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN empty because
the token-based publish is deprecated in favor of NPM OIDC trusted
publishers. Fix:

- Remove NODE_AUTH_TOKEN env var (conflicts with OIDC)
- Upgrade npm to latest (>=11.5.1) for OIDC publishing support
- Keep id-token: write permission and --provenance flag

NPM trusted publisher is already configured on npmjs.com for this
repo. OIDC federates identity from GitHub Actions → NPM, eliminating
the need for long-lived tokens.

* fix: payload passthrough for wing/room/original_text/valid_until in retrieval pipeline

Root cause: hybrid_search._vector_search() and memory_retrieval.retrieve_context()
were both hardcoded to cherry-pick only content/type/project/tags/timestamp from
Qdrant payload results. All spatial, temporal, and zero-loss fields were silently
dropped. Also fixed retrieve command threshold default (0.7 -> 0.45) and removed
stale imports causing UnboundLocalError in contradiction resolver.

* fix: complete contradiction resolver pipeline

Two root causes prevented contradiction resolution from working:
1. Empty query string in retrieve_context() produced zero-score vectors
   that fell below threshold, so no existing facts were ever found.
   Fix: use the new content as the search query.
2. Deprecated facts (valid_until set) were never filtered during search.
   Fix: add must_not range filter in hybrid_search._vector_search() to
   auto-exclude points where valid_until <= current time.

* fix: BM25 results now respect Qdrant payload filters (project/wing/room)

BM25 (SQLite FTS5) has no concept of Qdrant payload filters, so unscoped
results leaked into hybrid search when: 1) BM25-only results merged into
vector results without validation, and 2) the BM25-only fallback path
(when vector returns 0) returned results without any filter check.

Fix: Both paths now validate each BM25-only result against Qdrant via
a point lookup before including it. Also fixed the merge path to pass
wing/room/original_text/valid_until through the merged result dict.

    maturityScoring, usageAnalysis, utils/*)
  - Runs 9 explicit unit test files (no shell globs for cross-platform safety)
  - coverageAnalysis: off with command runner (node:test built-in)
  - concurrency: 2, timeoutMS: 15000
  - Threshold break: 0 (informational during rollout)
  - HTML report written to reports/mutation/report.html
- Add test:mutation npm script: compile-tests then npx stryker run
- Add mutation-testing CI job in ci.yml:
  - Only runs on pull_request events (not push to main)
  - Depends on build job (tests must pass first)
  - continue-on-error: true to keep it non-blocking
  - Hard cap at 20 minutes
  - Uploads HTML report artifact (14-day retention)
- Add .stryker-tmp and reports/mutation/ to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mutation-tests): add inPlace:true to fix sandbox out/ directory issue

Stryker by default copies only git-tracked files to its sandbox.
Since \out/\ is gitignored, the compiled test files and vscode-shim
were missing from the sandbox, causing every test command to hang
until Stryker's per-mutant timeout was reached.

Setting inPlace:true makes Stryker mutate the compiled JS files
directly in the working directory (no sandbox), which works correctly
since npm run compile-tests already runs before npx stryker run.
Stryker still backs up the originals in .stryker-tmp before each
mutation and restores them afterwards.

Verified locally: html.js run completed in 5s with 0 timeouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make mutation-testing step non-blocking via step-level continue-on-error

Job-level continue-on-error keeps the CI workflow green but still marks
the mutation-testing job check as FAILURE. Adding continue-on-error at
the step level ensures the job itself succeeds even when Stryker times
out, so the check shows SUCCESS and doesn't block PR merges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: reduce mutation scope to stay within CI time budget

Remove usageAnalysis.js (1905 lines) and maturityScoring.js (1212 lines)
from the mutate list — together they generate ~5000+ mutations and push
total run time well past the 20-minute CI timeout.

Keep the focused utility files (tokenEstimation, sessionParser, dayKeys,
errors, html) which produce a manageable ~800 mutations.

Also increase concurrency 2→4 to cut run time roughly in half.

Overhauled styles for GettingStarted_1_0: updated theme variables, refreshed header gradient, refined spacing/typography, improved form grid and input focus states, added responsive breakpoints, introduced .gs-step-dots and .gs-submit-row, and restyled the primary continue button. fix: use consistent per-product VWAP benchmark across all metrics (#16)

profit_factor, daily_pnl(), best/worst trade selection, and the HTML
report trade table were all benchmarking fills against the portfolio-level
VWAP whilst total_alpha_eur used per-product VWAPs. This caused logically
contradictory output (e.g. +784 EUR Total PnL alongside Profit Factor 0.00).

Changes:
- compute_fill_pnl(): add optional product_vwaps dict; uses per-product
  benchmark when provided, falls back to portfolio VWAP
- compute_profit_factor(): thread product_vwaps through to compute_fill_pnl
- BacktestResult: add product_vwaps field (populated for IDC runs)
- BacktestResult.daily_pnl(): use product_vwaps via compute_fill_pnl
- BacktestResult.summary(): best/worst trade display uses product_vwaps
- BacktestEngine: pass per_product_vwap to compute_profit_factor and
  best/worst fill selection; store on BacktestResult
- report._top_trades_rows(): use result.product_vwaps

Tests:
- TestComputeFillPnl: per-product lookup, fallback to portfolio VWAP
- TestComputeProfitFactor: consistent benchmark, no-losses case
- test_profit_factor_consistent_with_total_pnl_sign: end-to-end IDC
